skill.name: paper-web
skill.description: Converts academic papers (LaTeX or PDF) into interactive websites, presentation videos, and conference posters using the Paper2All pipeline. Suitable for paper dissemination, conference preparation, creating explorable academic homepages, generating video abstracts, or producing printable posters.
skill.summary: Convert academic papers into websites, videos, and posters with the Paper2All pipeline
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 282 patterns across 7 files, predominantly false positives from markdown code-fence syntax and documentation keywords. The skill is a documentation-first workflow that instructs users how to run the Paper2All academic paper conversion pipeline. All shell commands are user-invoked installation/usage instructions, not auto-executing scripts. API key configuration is standard .env-file setup with placeholder values. The skill contains no executable code, obfuscation, or malicious intent. Risk factors (external_commands, network, env_access) are legitimate for a pipeline-wrapper skill and pose low risk in this documentation-only context.

medium_findings.title: API Key Configuration in Environment Files
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to create .env files containing API keys (OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY) for the Paper2All pipeline. These are standard placeholder instructions with example values such as 'your_openai_api_key_here'. The skill itself does not read or transmit these keys, but users should be aware that Claude may access .env file contents if instructed to read them during troubleshooting.

medium_findings.title: System Package Installation with Elevated Privileges
medium_findings.description: The installation guide instructs users to run 'sudo apt-get install libreoffice poppler-utils' on Linux systems. This is standard installation documentation for required system dependencies (LibreOffice for document conversion, Poppler for PDF processing). The sudo command is contextualized within platform-specific setup instructions and is expected for system package management.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Multiple URLs are embedded in the skill documentation, including GitHub repository links, software download pages, and the K-Dense Web promotional link. All URLs point to legitimate, well-known destinations relevant to the skill's purpose. No evidence of malicious redirects or data exfiltration endpoints.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Syntax Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged markdown code block fences (```bash, ```json, ```text) as shell backtick execution across all files. These are documentation formatting elements used to display code examples, not actual command execution. All 180+ external_commands flags are false positives from this pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Markdown Code Fences as Shell Backticks
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer incorrectly identified markdown code block delimiters (```bash, ```text, ```json) as Ruby/shell backtick execution syntax. This pattern accounts for approximately 180 of the 282 flagged issues. All instances are standard markdown formatting in documentation files, not executable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Documentation Keywords as Cryptographic Patterns
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged documentation keywords (section headings, YAML descriptions, format names like 'IMRAD') as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are plain English text in markdown documentation with no cryptographic code present. This accounts for approximately 45 of the 282 flagged issues.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Diagnostic Commands as Reconnaissance
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged system diagnostic commands (nvidia-smi, libreoffice --version, pdftoppm -v) in the troubleshooting section as system reconnaissance. These are standard verification commands documented to help users confirm their installation, not reconnaissance tools.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Analyzer Heuristic False Positive: Combined Risk Factor Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer's heuristic flagged a critical combination of code execution, network access, and credential access. In context, this skill is a documentation-first set of instructions for using an academic paper conversion tool. The skill contains no executable code. All patterns are documentation artifacts: shell command examples for running the pipeline, URLs for tool repositories, and .env file setup instructions with placeholder values.

content.user_title: Convert Academic Papers into Websites, Videos, and Posters
content.value_statement: Manually creating websites, videos, and posters for every paper is time-consuming and requires design skills most researchers lack. This skill guides you through using the Paper2All pipeline to automatically convert LaTeX or PDF papers into interactive websites, presentation videos, and print-ready conference posters.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through converting LaTeX or PDF papers into interactive academic websites with responsive layouts
Provides step-by-step instructions for generating presentation videos with narration and slides from paper content
Walks through creating print-ready conference posters with customizable dimensions and professional templates
Covers batch processing of multiple papers for lab or department-wide conversion projects
Documents API configuration for OpenAI, OpenRouter, and Google Search integration
Explains quality assessment and troubleshooting for all three output formats
limitations: This skill provides instructions only and does not include packaged executable scripts within the skill itself
Complex mathematical formulas may require manual verification after automated conversion
Video generation with talking-head feature requires an NVIDIA GPU with 48GB VRAM
Processing time ranges from 10 minutes to 2 hours per paper depending on components and paper length

use_cases.title: Conference Presenter Preparing Materials
use_cases.description: A researcher with an accepted conference paper needs a poster, a video abstract, and a companion website. The skill walks through generating all three from a single LaTeX source, with conference-specific dimensions and branding.

use_cases.title: Lab Director Promoting Group Publications
use_cases.description: A lab director wants to create websites for all papers published by the group this year. The skill guides batch processing of multiple papers to generate a consistent set of explorable homepages for the lab website.

use_cases.title: Graduate Student Creating a Preprint Homepage
use_cases.description: A PhD student uploading a paper to arXiv or bioRxiv wants to add an interactive homepage to increase visibility. The skill provides quick-start instructions for converting a PDF or LaTeX source into a professional website in under 30 minutes.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a full conference package
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to prepare a complete set of presentation materials for my conference paper. The LaTeX source is in the folder 'papers/my-paper/'. Please guide me through generating an interactive website, a 48x36 inch poster, and a 12-minute presentation video.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher preparing all materials for an upcoming conference

prompt_templates.title: Convert a PDF paper to an interactive website
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a published PDF paper at 'papers/published-paper.pdf' that I want to promote online. Walk me through the steps to convert it into an interactive academic homepage with responsive design and citation integration.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher promoting a published paper online

prompt_templates.title: Create a video abstract for journal submission
prompt_templates.prompt: My journal requires a 5-minute video abstract for my submission. I have the LaTeX source in 'papers/journal-paper/'. Guide me through generating a professional video abstract with slides, narration, and clear visual presentation of key findings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Author preparing a video abstract for journal requirements

prompt_templates.title: Batch process multiple papers for a lab website
prompt_templates.prompt: Our research group has 5 papers from this year in the 'papers/batch/' folder. I need to generate websites for all of them with consistent branding and institutional logos. Walk me through the batch processing setup and deployment options.
prompt_templates.scenario: Lab manager generating websites for multiple publications

output_examples.input: Convert my LaTeX paper at 'papers/neurips-paper/' into an interactive website
output: The Paper2Web pipeline will process your LaTeX source and generate an interactive website with these sections: abstract, key findings, methodology overview, results with interactive figures, and references with DOI links. Here is the step-by-step workflow to set up the pipeline, configure your API keys, run the conversion, and deploy the generated website to GitHub Pages or your academic hosting service.

output_examples.input: Generate a 48x36 inch poster from my paper for the upcoming conference
output: Paper2Poster will create a print-ready conference poster at your specified dimensions. The workflow covers installing dependencies, organizing your LaTeX source, running the poster generation command with custom dimensions, and quality checks to perform before sending to print. The output includes a PDF ready for professional printing with 300 DPI resolution.

best_practices: Use LaTeX source files (not PDF) when available for the best content extraction and structural accuracy
Organize all paper assets (figures, tables, bibliography) in a single directory before starting the conversion pipeline
Verify generated output quality on multiple devices before finalizing websites, posters, or videos for publication
anti_patterns: Running the pipeline on scanned PDFs without selectable text will produce poor results and should be avoided
Skipping the quality review step after generation often leads to unnoticed formatting errors or broken links
Using the talking-head video feature without verifying GPU requirements (48GB VRAM minimum) will cause the process to fail

faq.question: What input formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: The Paper2All pipeline accepts LaTeX source files (recommended) and high-quality PDFs with selectable text. LaTeX provides the best content extraction and structure preservation.

faq.question: How long does it take to convert a paper?
faq.answer: Website generation takes 15 to 30 minutes per paper. Poster generation takes 10 to 20 minutes. Video generation takes 20 to 60 minutes without talking-head, or 60 to 120 minutes with talking-head enabled.

faq.question: Do I need a GPU to use this skill?
faq.answer: A GPU is only required for the optional talking-head video feature, which needs an NVIDIA A6000 with 48GB VRAM. All other components (website, poster, standard video) run on CPU.

faq.question: What does it cost to run the pipeline?
faq.answer: Estimated API costs are: 0.50 to 2.00 USD per website, 0.30 to 1.00 USD per poster, 1.00 to 3.00 USD per video. A complete package costs approximately 2.00 to 6.00 USD per paper when using GPT-4.

faq.question: Can I batch process multiple papers at once?
faq.answer: Yes, the pipeline supports batch processing. Organize each paper in its own subdirectory under a common input folder and run the pipeline once. Papers are processed sequentially by default or in parallel using shell scripting.

faq.question: What quality checks should I perform after generation?
faq.answer: For websites, test on multiple devices and verify all links. For posters, print a test page and check readability from 3 to 6 feet. For videos, watch the complete output and verify audio-video synchronization.
